macro_summary: Monday's session saw tech stocks hammered, with the Nasdaq plunging 4.12% and SOXX crashing 8.71% after AVGO's AI chip forecast miss last week triggered a broader semis rout. Oil surged 3.3% to $93.54 as Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday, escalating the Hormuz crisis and adding a geopolitical risk premium ahead of this week's CPI (Wednesday) and FOMC (next week). The VIX spiked 40% to 21.51, reflecting extreme fear.
commodities_note: WTI rose to $93.54 (+3.3%) on renewed Iran-Israel military escalation; gold slipped 0.4% to $4,346.5 as the US dollar firmed (DXY 100.09). The US 10-year yield climbed to 4.54%, continuing its hawkish march after last Friday's blowout payrolls report. No data anomalies on benchmarks.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks upgraded to WATCH as RSI cools from overbought levels. The NIM compression headwind persists with US10Y at 4.54% (well above the 4.20% reset) and no MAS easing until October. UOB trades at a discount to cluster median, while DBS offers the highest dividend yield. The cluster remains a waiting game for either a US rate break or a domestic policy shift.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — NIM relief distant but valuations attractive.
singapore.key_development: RSI normalization across the board allows an upgrade to WATCH, but no fundamental catalyst has changed.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX plunged 8.7% on Monday, extending Friday's AVGO-driven rout. Only NVDA and TSM remain at WATCH — NVDA is at its 50-day, TSM is structurally underpinned by CoWoS. All other semis are hard-blocked on extension or over-valued. The cluster is in a risk-off correction, not a dip-buying opportunity; wait for a washout before adding.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically crumbling — wait for washout.
semiconductors.key_development: AVGO's AI chip forecast miss continues to reverberate, with indiscriminate selling erasing the Computex premium.

big_tech.summary: GOOG and MSFT upgraded to WATCH as they approach their 50-day averages, with MSFT's in-house AI pivot a positive thesis development. AMZN holds below its 50-day amid job cuts and capex scrutiny, while PLTR remains CAUTION on extreme valuation. The cluster is bifurcated: cloud AI spending is still massive, but near-term rate and demand fears cap upside.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautiously constructive — AI capex is the load-bearing leg, but valuations need to reset.
big_tech.key_development: Berkshire's $10B Alphabet bet validates AI infrastructure demand, but the broader selloff overrides.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Direct beneficiary of NVDA's $2B photonics investment; paired with LITE, optical interconnects are the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters. The semis rout presents a potential pullback opportunity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels following the semis rout, aligning with a re-test of the 50-day SMA.

interconnected.name: Lam Research
interconnected.reason: Chip equipment supplier directly exposed to US-China export curbs and the semis capex cycle; key read on ASML and TSM demand. Mentioned in today's news per Simply Wall St. on the impact of new export restrictions.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Wednesday's CPI if semis selloff deepens, targeting a 15-20% pullback from pre-rout highs for a contrarian equipment play.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO, benefiting from the same hyperscaler shift to custom silicon. A cheaper alternative to play AI infrastructure if AVGO stabilizes.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after MU earnings on June 25 for memory pricing confirmation and AI capex trends; add on a 10% pullback if semis continue to correct.

active_risks: Iran-Israel conflict escalation: Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday, threatening to keep Strait of Hormuz closed indefinitely; oil surges (per WSJ, Jun 7).
Strait of Hormuz blockade: Day 97 of disrupted shipping, WTI back to $93.54; no ceasefire in sight.
US-China tech tensions: Potential new export controls on chip equipment (per Simply Wall St., Jun 7); tariff proposals on 60 nations add trade friction.
Upcoming ECB rate hike: Expected Thursday despite eurozone contraction, adding to global tightening pressure.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil spike is punishing tech (semis -8.7%) and benefiting defense (PLTR). Higher yields (US10Y 4.54%) pressure growth stocks. Singapore banks are range-bound as NIM headwind persists.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran launched missiles at Israel, escalating the Hormuz crisis and sending WTI >$93. NVDA/SK Hynix multiyear partnership announced (per Reuters).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold defensive positions; no new entries until VIX drops below 22 and semis show signs of stabilization. Cash is a position.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — market under severe stress, semis free-falling.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 10 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The semis and BigTech clusters are in correction mode, and the Hormuz escalation adds oil price uncertainty, keeping VIX elevated. No compelling entries across the watchlist.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
